threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it ‘the Tube’ instead of just ‘transport’ like us locals. You’ll get there, just gotta ditch the tourist lingo!
Saw a bloke on the 35 last night openly reading a script for a play. He looked so serious, but no one else seemed to care. Just another Brixton night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a family hosting a barbecue in the middle of a street party while a bus somehow squeezed through—it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a massive mural of a cat wearing sunglasses next to a vegan café named after a famous philosopher. Pure Berlin!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they know the ‘secret’ taco truck by the empty lot on 24th—struction workers swear by their salsa.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a dog lounging on the tracks at Civic Center. Muni's delays are a staple, but a pooch casually blocking the train? Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all trendy bars; they miss the quiet corners where locals sip café amb llet and gossip in Catalan.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they stare blankly at the metro map, still clutching it like a lifeline—just ask any old-timer.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the tube' instead of just 'the overground' like the rest of us. They'll know you're fresh off the train.
Spotted a bloke in a Hawaiian shirt arguing with a pigeon outside the Ritzy at 2am, claiming it owed him money. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Realized I was home when a guy in flip-flops tried to haggle over a bus ticket with the BVG inspector at Hermannplatz.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a late-night waffle truck parked outside a techno club, serving fresh waffles with ketchup. It's a vibe, trust me.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local when they complain about the 'new' taco truck on 24th refusing to serve al pastor after 10 PM.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tutu dancing on the bus as it crawled past the Civic Center. Only in SF do delays inspire performances like that.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists keep asking for gluten-free 'pa de pessic' but miss the joy of a local 'coca de recapte'—try the simple joys!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newcomers by their reluctance to step off the sidewalk when a local is approaching. They still think space is shareable.
